transcript: [accented] Yes, that is the number, man. Rodney, man.
No. No. Nobody here by that name.
[accented] No, the, um... the Nugent records.
I have no idea what you're talking about.
[accented] The Nugent records, man. The Nugent albums, man.
I don't know what you're talkin' about.
[accented] Man... Rodney's Nugent tape, man. You know, the Nugent... Ted Nugent?
[accented] He got that tape of Nugent doin'...
No, you got the wrong number, man.
[accented] No, man, come on. Please. The Nugent record. It's the one record, man, I need to get from him.
[accented] No, Ran... Rodney, Randy, you know, whoever, you know, got the Nugent.
Well, nobody's here by that name.
[accented] You got that Nugent tape, man?
No, I sure don't. I don't even have a tape player.
I don't even have a tape player.
[sigh] [accented] What am I supposed to do about this record, bro?
[accented] Well, what do you recommend, bro?
I don't know, um.... Should try a record store... [laughing] Or, I don't know what to tell you. You've got the wrong number.
Yeah, you've got the wrong number.
[accented] Rodney lives at eight-six- uh... fuckin' zero-seven-six, uh... two-oh-six or whatever, you know? He got that Nugent tape on disc, you know?
I don't know. I don't know what to tell you.
This-- uh, listen, I've got somebody on the line. I gotta go. You got the wrong number, okay?
[accented] Well, it's... it's bad when he gets into that funk, you know? Nugent.
Um... well, listen. I'm sorry. You've got the wrong number. Call... call somebody else.
